assertions.title: HFT Edge graph node and surface exist

steps.action: Open the BETTER atlas root surface
steps.expected: The graph shows an HFT Edge node
steps.observed: The root atlas shows an interactive "HFT Edge" node/button alongside the other graph destinations.

steps.expected: The intended HFT detail surface opens
steps.observed: The page switched to a detail state labeled "BETTER Atlas → HFT Edge" with an "HFT Edge" heading and latency-moat summary.

evidence.network: No assertion-specific API/XHR requests were needed; the browser only fetched the local Next.js app and a client chunk during navigation.

steps.action: Inspect the HFT Edge detail surface text
steps.expected: The surface names Rust execution, same-rack co-location, and a 0.11ms vs 8ms comparison
steps.observed: The HFT surface explicitly states Rust execution, same AWS rack co-location with the Polymarket CLOB, and "0.11ms vs 8ms" latency framing.

evidence.network: No relevant API calls; assertion validated from visible copy on the local HFT surface.

steps.action: Inspect the Z-Score and pipeline sections on HFT Edge
steps.expected: The surface includes Z-Score, 35-70x returns, Sharpe >40, FAST15M, LONG, Kelly sizing, and ~60x ROI
steps.observed: The HFT surface includes a Z-Score section with "35-70x" and "Sharpe >40", plus FAST15M and LONG BRAID lanes, Kelly sizing, and a "~60x" paper-trade ROI callout.

evidence.network: No relevant API calls; assertion validated from visible copy on the local HFT surface.

steps.action: Inspect the copy-trading distribution section on HFT Edge
steps.expected: The surface describes gas-sponsored execution, UDA, and one-click copy trading
steps.observed: The HFT surface explicitly lists "gas-sponsored", "UDA", and "one-click copy trading" in the copy-trading distribution section.

evidence.network: No relevant API calls; assertion validated from visible copy on the local HFT surface.

steps.action: Open the Proof & Trust surface from the atlas and inspect the proof copy
steps.expected: The proof surface includes terminal copy-trading details for gas-sponsored execution, UDA, and one-click
steps.observed: The Proof & Trust surface shows a "Terminal execution abstraction" section with bullets for gas-sponsored execution, UDA (Unified Deposit Account), and one-click copy trading.

evidence.network: Observed a local Next.js client-chunk fetch during surface navigation; no assertion-specific API/XHR traffic was required.

summary: Tested 5 assigned assertions on the running local app: VAL-CONTENT-005, 006, 007, 008, and 016 all passed. The HFT Edge node opens the intended detail surface, and both the HFT and Proof surfaces visibly contain the required Rust/co-location/latency, pipeline, and copy-trading details.
